summary: Comprehensive testing of StockHub SaaS application - Auth, Suppliers, Dashboard endpoints and Frontend UI. All 19 backend API tests passed. Frontend tests verified login page, registration flow, dashboard stats, and supplier CRUD operations.

minor.issue: FTP connection to non-existent hosts causes 60-second timeout, resulting in Cloudflare 520 errors. The fix for known hosts that deny access (returning JSON error status 200) works correctly.
Warning: HMAC key is 24 bytes (below recommended 32 bytes for SHA256). Consider using a longer JWT_SECRET.

passed_tests: Backend: User registration duplicate email rejection
Backend: Create supplier with FTP config (POST /api/suppliers)
Backend: Sync supplier without FTP config returns 400
Backend: Sync supplier with FTP error returns JSON error status
Frontend: Login page renders correctly with email, password inputs and submit button
Frontend: Registration flow with all required fields (name, email, password, confirm password)
Frontend: Dashboard loads with all stat cards (Proveedores, Productos, Catálogo, Stock Bajo, Sin Stock, Cambios de Precio)
Frontend: Add Supplier dialog opens with tabs (General, Conexión, Config. CSV)
Frontend: Supplier creation works and shows success toast
critical_code_review_comments: Consider increasing JWT_SECRET length to at least 32 bytes for better security
FTP sync operations should implement a shorter timeout (10-15s) to avoid Cloudflare gateway timeouts
seed_data_creation: Created test users with unique emails (ui_test_{uuid}@example.com) and TEST_UISupplier_{uuid} suppliers during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests are in /app/backend/tests/test_api_endpoints.py. All auth, supplier CRUD, sync, and dashboard endpoints work correctly. Frontend registration requires confirm password field. The column_mapping field in suppliers endpoint was fixed (previously was incorrectly named csv_field_mapping).
rca_of_the_issue: The original bug mentioned by main agent (csv_field_mapping vs column_mapping) was already fixed. The sync endpoint now returns JSON with error status instead of throwing HTTP 500 exceptions. Testing confirmed these fixes are working correctly.
